pattern: After sorting both robots and factories by position, an optimal assignment never crosses, so a dynamic program over robots and factories with a per-factory capacity loop minimizes total travel.
hints: An optimal matching of points on a line never has two assignments cross, so sort robots and factories first.
Process factories one at a time, deciding how many of the next consecutive robots each factory takes.
Let dp[i][j] be the min distance to assign the first i robots using the first j factories; transition by trying that factory repair the next t robots up to its limit, paying the summed distances.

edge_inputs.description: factory at same spot as a robot costs zero for it
limited capacity forces a far factory to help
all robots on one side of all factories

stress.description: random robots and factories with enough total capacity to repair everyone
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = max(1, size)
    f = max(1, size // 3)
    robots = rng.sample(range(-500, 500), m)
    fpos = rng.sample(range(-500, 500), f)
    cap_each = -(-m // f) + 1
    factory = [[p, rng.randint(cap_each, cap_each + 2)] for p in fpos]
    return [robots, factory]
stress.note: capacities chosen so total limit comfortably covers all robots
